summary: Large lbRefract decompilation/matching PR with small lbMthp prototype fallout. It replaced raw/global placeholder access in lbrefract with typed structs, real GX/HSD includes, several matched texture read/write and refraction setup functions, class initialization for refract DObj/PObj types, and a more structured projection texture-matrix path. The decomp.dev report credited 9 new matches and +1848 matched code bytes, while noting one broken .sdata match.
what_changed: Implemented or significantly improved lbRefract functions including lbRefract_80021CE8, lbRefract_800222A4, lbRefract_PObjLoad, and lbRefract_80022998.
Added matched static helpers fn_80021F70, fn_80021FF8, fn_8002206C, fn_80022650, and fn_80022940 according to the decomp.dev report.
Changed lbRefract_8002219C width/height parameters from u16 to s32 internally and simplified the unsupported-format switch path to use break plus a single success return.
Replaced raw static int lbl_804336D0[0x10] with a typed static struct lbl_804336D0_t containing refractionUserCount, image_ptr, unk_8, unk_C, and texture_mtx, with a STATIC_ASSERT size of 0x40.
Introduced struct lbRefract_DataLayout for lbl_803BB0B0 with texture matrices, HSD image/texture descriptors, DObj/PObj class info, and library/class name strings.
Reworked lbRefract_8002247C to access typed HSD_CObj projection fields instead of M2C_FIELD offsets, and to write into lbl_804336D0.texture_mtx.
Implemented refraction texture allocation/loading flow using lbArchive_LoadSymbols, HSD_MemAlloc, memset, GXGetTexBufferSize, HSD_TObjLoadDesc, and DCFlushRange.
Added PObj load hook logic that calculates vertex-display stride and copies PNMTXIDX data into another display-list offset for PObjs flagged with 0x2000, then flushes the display buffer.
Implemented lbRefract_80022998 GX setup for indirect texture refraction: texture generators, texture matrices, TEV, indirect texture stage, Z/blend/alpha/dither state.
Updated lbRefract_80022998 declaration in lbrefract.h so the third parameter is s32 instead of s8.
Updated THPDec_80331340 declaration and call sites to take an additional s32 width argument in lbmthp.h and lbmthp.c.
Changed lbRefract_80022BD0 underflow handling from OSReport plus __assert to HSD_ASSERTREPORT with the existing error text.
smart_moves: Typing lbl_804336D0 converted repeated magic-index access into named fields such as refractionUserCount and image_ptr, making later matching and review of side effects much safer.
Using real HSD_CObj projection union fields in lbRefract_8002247C removed fragile M2C_FIELD offset reads while preserving the three projection cases.
Adding explicit GX/HSD includes allowed functions to use named GX enums and HSD structures instead of broad unknown placeholders.
The lbRefract_DataLayout struct captured a large data blob with offsets and embedded class-info/name fields, which is useful for matching data layout and class init code together.
The PR matched small helper functions first, especially texture packing/unpacking callbacks, giving lbRefract_8002219C and later refraction generation code concrete callback signatures.
The math-heavy lbRefract_80021CE8 used __frsqrte refinement and a volatile stack temporary, a common tactic for matching PowerPC floating-point sqrt-like codegen.
The PObj load implementation computes display-list stride from GX vertex descriptors rather than hardcoding one stride, which grounds the copy loop in the actual vertex format.
The THPDec_80331340 signature update fixed a near-match in lbmthp by passing width through both affected call sites.
decomp_lessons: When a global is accessed by multiple nearby functions, promote the raw array to a named struct and guard the inferred size with STATIC_ASSERT before expanding behavior around it.
For HSD camera/projection code, prefer known struct fields such as cobj->projection_param.* and cobj->near over M2C_FIELD once headers support the layout.
Texture format callbacks benefit from precise function pointer signatures; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with typed callback arguments can unlock surrounding switch/function matches.
GX texture-buffer code often needs explicit casting at API boundaries: this PR keeps lbRefract_8002219C width/height as s32 while casting to u16 for GXGetTexBufferSize.
PowerPC floating-point matching may require explicit __frsqrte Newton refinement and volatile stack temporaries to reproduce original register/stack behavior.
Data and code matching are coupled: this PR gained many code matches but the bot reported .sdata regressed from 100% to 28.57%, so data placement/static storage changes should be checked separately.
Class-info setup functions can be small, high-value matches: initialize with hsdInitClassInfo, then assign the overridden method pointer such as DObj disp or PObj load.
Display-list mutation code should flush the affected buffer with DCFlushRange after writes.
naming_conventions: Known library functions use lbRefract_* and lbMthp* prefixes.
Still-unknown local helpers retain address-style names such as fn_80021F70, fn_80021FF8, fn_8002206C, fn_80022650, and fn_80022940.
Global labels retain linker-style names such as lbl_803BB0B0, lbl_804336D0, lbl_804D63E8, and lbl_803BB0E0 when semantic names are not fully established.
Typed wrapper structs can use label-derived names when semantics remain partial, as with struct lbl_804336D0_t.
More semantic field names were introduced where the purpose was clear, including refractionUserCount, image_ptr, texture_mtx, lib_name, dobj_name, and pobj_name.
Unknown fields remain marked with unk_* names, e.g. unk_8 and unk_C, rather than guessing.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(20) in lbRefract_80021CE8 to influence stack layout.
Used __frsqrte and repeated Newton-Raphson refinement to match sqrt-distance code.
Used a volatile f32 local in lbRefract_80021CE8 to force a float store/load pattern.
Preserved tiled texture addressing formulas for IA4/RGB565/RGBA8 callbacks using row/col bit masks such as col >> 2, row & 3, row * 8 & 0xFFFFFFE0, and row_stride alignment.
Replaced goto-based switch success exits in lbRefract_8002219C with breaks plus a common return while still reaching 100% according to the bot.
Matched projection matrix setup by mapping projection-type cases to MTXLightPerspective, MTXLightFrustum, and MTXLightOrtho with explicit constants 0.5F and -0.5F.
Matched GX state setup in lbRefract_80022998 with named enum calls for texgen, TEV, indirect texture, Z mode, blend mode, alpha compare, and dither.
Display-list copy loop uses command/count parsing and an unrolled copy path for count > 8, then flushes with DCFlushRange.
Function signature correction for THPDec_80331340 added the width argument to recover fn_8001EF5C's final 1-byte mismatch.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body only said: "Tracking PR as usual".
decomp.dev reported matched code 62.46% (+0.05%, +1848 bytes) and matched data 38.04% (-0.00%, -8 bytes).
decomp.dev reported 9 new matches: lbRefract_80022998, fn_8002206C, fn_80021FF8, fn_80022650, fn_80022940, fn_80021F70, lbRefract_8002219C, lbRefract_8002247C, and lbmthp fn_8001EF5C.
decomp.dev reported 6 improvements in unmatched items, including lbRefract_80021CE8 to 98.12%, lbRefract_PObjLoad to 70.48%, and lbRefract_800222A4 to 84.44%.
decomp.dev reported one broken match: main/melee/lb/lbrefract .sdata dropped from 100.00% to 28.57%.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for whether lbl_804336D0_t fields unk_8 and unk_C were given semantic names.
Search for follow-up fixes to lbRefract .sdata after the bot-reported broken match.
Look for later work completing lbRefract_80021CE8, lbRefract_PObjLoad, and lbRefract_800222A4 from partial to full matches.
Verify whether THPDec_80331340's fifth width parameter was propagated to its implementation or external declaration elsewhere.
Search later PRs for additional lbrefract data layout improvements around lbl_803BB0B0 and refract_class_library.
